title: chenopodium × trigonocarpum
intro: Chenopodium × trigonocarpum, also known as the trigonocarp chenopod, is a hybrid plant species within the Chenopodium genus, belonging to the family Amaranthaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating digestive issues. The health benefits of Chenopodium × trigonocarpum include its ability to act as an antioxidant, reduce oxidative stress, and support immune function. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant can be used as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and its leaves can also be dried and used in salves or poultices for topical application.
